desc: DAG expansion protocol — adversarial hardening: spec-first bugs, property tests, consumer validation, plan mode

init.desc: Library core + seed tests + self-referential roadmap + expansion skill

fr-predicates.desc: Cross-repo predicates: siblingArtifactExists(root), gitArtifactAt(root, ref), any() OR combinator

fr-cross-orient.desc: Async crossOrient: read .roadmap.json deps, parallel sibling orient, blockedBy field; fix orderByDependencies transitive BFS; handle missing dep repos gracefully

fr-cli-surface.desc: CLI: chart --deps (cross-repo progress), merge --from (diagnostic reconcile), trail dep context in breadcrumbs

fr-consumer-unblock.desc: E2E validation: mono-fusion scenario — orient with blockedBy, chart --deps, merge --from ../sibling, trail deps, configurable siblingPath

reorient.desc: Session entry gate: run boot.ts, verify orientation, confirm position, choose mode

adv-reconcile.desc: Adversarial spec: reconcile gap.missing = unmet consumes only, not surplus produces

adv-orient.desc: Adversarial spec: orient empty-produces stalls permanently — specify correct behavior

adv-property.desc: Property-based: for all valid graphs, order()→orient() consistent, check()→verify() agree

adv-types.desc: Type-level: invalid dep refs, id/key mismatch, unknown nodes are tsc errors

consumer-integration.desc: Consumer smoke test: install from path, write minimal roadmap.ts, orient() from real filesystem

phase-1-term.desc: Phase 1 complete: adversarially hardened protocol core (bugs fixed, contracts proven)

merge-spec.desc: Spec: merge(g1, g2, connections) combines DAGs at reconcile() join points — init/term unification strategy

adv-merge.desc: Adversarial spec: merge() preserves structure (no cycles), unifies nodes correctly, consumes satisfied

merge-impl.desc: Implement merge(g1, g2): validate inputs, reconcile(), add structural edges, define() + verify() merged graph

phase-2-term.desc: Phase 2 complete: DAG merge operations enable recursive expansion + multi-repo coordination

branch-spec.desc: Spec: branch(g, from) extracts subgraph from node to term, creates variant DAG for parallel development

adv-branch.desc: Adversarial spec: branch() preserves structure (acyclic), includes all reachable nodes to term, consumes satisfied

branch-impl.desc: Implement branch(g, from): extract subgraph, set new init/term, validate via define() + verify()

phase-3-term.desc: Phase 3 complete: branch operations enable parallel development + variant exploration

predicate-builders.desc: Curried exists predicates: fileExists(root), gitArtifactExists(root), compound(...preds)

parallel-order.desc: parallelOrder(g) → string[][] — batches of mutually independent nodes for concurrent execution

error-model.desc: RoadmapError class: typed codes (POSITION_MISMATCH, CONTRACT_VIOLATION, ...) + fix field + entry point

phase-13-term.desc: Phase 13 complete: Consumption surface (CLI, predicates, parallelOrder, errors, clean docs, file headers, CLAUDE.md)

term.desc: v0.5.0-consumption-ready: All phases complete, CLI binary ships, clean API surface

file-headers.desc: Structured @module/@exports/@types/@entry headers in all src/ files. Enables grep-based API discovery.

repo-cleanup.desc: Remove stale .roadmap/ expansion scripts, tests/adoption/ scaffolding, dead artifacts from completed phases

claude-md.desc: Update .claude/CLAUDE.md for consumer LLMs: module map, export table, type signatures, usage examples

git-library.desc: Consolidate git operations: repoInfo, artifactAtRef, archivedFiles, fileHistory, restore, stageAndCommit, createBranch

consumer-categories.desc: Six consumer entry points: hooks, git, agent, developer, full, skills — each with targeted docs + API surface

git-ops-impl.desc: git-ops mechanical skill: read/write/log/diff/exists/list/commit operations. Cross-repo reads, local writes, atomic commits, --session-squash with env inference (CLAUDE_SESSION_ID or ROADMAP_SESSION_ID). Input via --stdin or --from-file.

migration-script.desc: Migration script for early adopters (Mac + Linux): symlink MEMORY.md → /dev/null, create .roadmap/memory/ locally, initialize ~/.roadmap/memory/ as standalone git repo. Idempotent and platform-aware.

memory-docs.desc: Document per-agent and per-swarm memory patterns: self-orientation on spawn, learned patterns as guards, session-local memory, cross-repo context. Per-swarm: collective memory synthesis, memory-driven coordination, swarm-specific optimizations, memory as audit trail. Include examples in TypeScript.

memory-tests.desc: Tests for git-ops skill (cross-repo reads, local write isolation, atomic commits, session squash, --stdin/--from-file) and migration script (idempotency, platform detection, symlink creation). Integration + unit tests.

phase-14-term.desc: Phase 14 complete: Agent memory infrastructure (git-ops skill, migration script, memory patterns, tests)

locate-readmaps.desc: Roadmap discovery skill: find all .roadmap/head.json files on machine, load+parse each, orient each one, return { name, path, dagId, position, complete, nodes, blockedBy }. Graceful failure on corrupted DAGs.

cli-cross-project.desc: CLI commands for cross-repo coordination: `roadmap locate --all` (discover), `roadmap sync [--format json|tree]` (aggregate tasks), `roadmap parallel --cross-repo [--graph]` (show parallel batches across repos). Enables agents to see all available work and parallel fanout points.

git-hook-test-enforcement.desc: Pre-commit hook enforcing test-writing: commit fails if src/*.ts changed but no tests/*.test.ts added. Allow bypass via SKIP_TEST_CHECK=<reason>. Store config in .roadmap/hook-config.json. Install via `roadmap install-hooks`. Configurable per-repo.

phase-15-term.desc: Phase 15 complete: Cross-repo coordination infrastructure (discovery, aggregation, parallel scheduling, test enforcement). Agents can now see all roadmaps and work across them.

batch-position-types.desc: Redefine Orientation type: position becomes string[] (batch, not node). Implement orient() returning batch, advanceBatch() for advancement. Breaking change to API. Updates: src/protocol.ts type system, orient() signature, new advanceBatch() function.

batch-validation-layer.desc: Add validateBatch() function to validate all nodes in a batch as unit. Check artifact materialization (all produces must exist). Ensures batch completeness before advancement. Updates: src/validation.ts, new validateBatch() export.

batch-cli-trail.desc: Update CLI commands: orient/advance for batch-level position. Update trail schema to record batch progressions (position: string[] instead of string). New cmdAdvance() function. Updates: bin/roadmap.ts, trail JSON format.

batch-enforcement-hook.desc: Update pre-commit hook for batch-level enforcement: prevent commits unless entire batch validates AND all artifacts materialized. New validation: batchComplete + artifact-exists checks. Updates: hooks/pre-commit script.

batch-position-tests.desc: Comprehensive test rewrite for batch semantics: orient() returns array, advanceBatch() validation, artifact materialization, batch completion. Rewrite protocol.test.ts, add batch-specific tests. Coverage: linear DAGs, diamond DAGs, complex DAGs.

phase-16-term.desc: Phase 16 complete: Batch-level position model enforced. Position is array (batch), not string (node). Cannot advance before entire batch complete. Trail records batch progressions. Pre-commit prevents partial batch commits. Breaking API change, but mechanically enforced.

plan-mode-types.desc: Add mode?: 'execute' | 'plan' to NodeSpec, expandedFrom?: string for provenance, { type: 'expanded', minNodes?: number } to ValidationRule union. Optional fields — existing DAGs unchanged. Compile-time enforcement.

plan-mode-orient.desc: Update orient() plan node semantics: a node with mode='plan' is done when at least one node with expandedFrom pointing to it exists in the graph. If no expansion children exist, plan node is current position (needs expansion). Default mode='execute' preserves existing behavior.

plan-mode-validate.desc: Implement 'expanded' validation rule in validateNode: check that nodes with expandedFrom === nodeId exist in graph, count >= minNodes (default 1). Returns evidence listing expansion children found.

plan-mode-brief.desc: Add mode field to Brief type. getBrief reads node mode and returns it. inferPattern handles plan mode: 'Decompose into sub-tasks. Decide if human input needed. Output is DAG expansion.' Agents branch on brief.mode.

plan-mode-cli.desc: CLI plan mode surface: orient output includes mode field per node, chart marks plan nodes with distinct emoji (📋), describe shows plan vs execute node counts. Help text documents plan mode.

plan-mode-tests.desc: Comprehensive tests: mode field type validation (optional, defaults execute), orient with plan nodes (done when expanded, current when not), expanded validation rule (count children, evidence), Brief.mode field, CLI mode output. Edge cases: plan node with no children, plan node expanded into more plan nodes (recursive).

phase-17-term.desc: Phase 17 complete: Plan node execution mode operational. Nodes declare mode ('execute' | 'plan'), orient() handles plan completion via expansion detection, validateNode checks 'expanded' rule, Brief surfaces mode to agents, CLI displays plan nodes distinctly. Backward compatible — mode defaults to 'execute'.

shell-validate-rule.desc: Add shell ValidationRule type — { type: "shell", command, expectExitCode? }. Enables nodes to declare arbitrary shell commands as validation gates. Wired into validateNode and the validate CLI command.

silent-orient.desc: orient --check: silent, note-exempt batch polling for swarm agents. No trail entry written. Added to NOTE_EXEMPT gate and orient --check path. Prevents trail pollution from high-frequency position checks.

pending-contracts.desc: ConsumeSpec: string | { artifact, resolvedBy }. Acknowledged pending contracts suppress verify() warnings while the resolver node is still incomplete. Enables DAGs with intentional cross-node artifact handoffs without false-positive verify errors.

speckit-import.desc: roadmap import --from speckit <file.md> --id <dag-id>: parse a tasks.md into a roadmap DAG. parseTasksMd + tasksToDAG in src/lib/speckit-import.ts. CLI wired to cmdImport.

phase-18-term.desc: Phase 18 complete: shell validate rule, silent orient (--check), pending contracts (ConsumeSpec resolvedBy), and spec-kit import command all operational.

agent-dispatch-api.desc: readyNodes(): eager dispatch beyond current batch whose deps are met. nextBatch(): orchestrator lookahead with conflict pre-check. criticalPath(): longest-path init→term by node count. All exported from index. orient --ready and --next flags.

loop-signal-types.desc: loopTarget + convergenceCheck on NodeSpec. LoopSignal interface in Orientation — present when current batch contains a loop node. Structurally distinguishes terminal nodes from loop nodes. orient --check output includes loop field.

cli-agent-commands.desc: New CLI commands: show <node-id> (full node spec as JSON), commit --node <id> (stages produces + [node: X] trailer), checkpoint --label, diff. install-hooks wired to dispatch + extended to all four hooks (pre-commit, post-commit, commit-msg, prepare-commit-msg). Shell wrapper for .ts hook sources.

orient-staged-flag.desc: orient --staged: per-node isomorphism check — do staged files exactly match a node's produces? Returns { files, matches, extraFiles, isomorphic }. Enforces one-commit-per-node at the orient level rather than only at the pre-commit hook level.

phase-19-term.desc: Phase 19 complete: agent dispatch API (readyNodes/nextBatch/criticalPath), loop signal types, CLI agent commands (show/commit/checkpoint/diff/install-hooks), orient --staged isomorphism check all operational.

complete-command.desc: roadmap complete <node-id>: atomic claim → checkpoint → reorient → auto-advance when last in batch. --no-advance to suppress auto-advance. Eliminates 5-call agent sequence (claim + checkpoint + orient + advance + trail) to 1 call.

ambient-field.desc: NodeSpec.ambient: readonly string[] of files agent reads for context but that don't gate readiness or appear in dependency resolution. Decouples "I need this to read" from "I need this to unblock." Threaded through Flat type, surfaced in show output alongside consumes.

loop-validation.desc: convergenceCheck schema enforcement: define() rejects unknown keys (valid: maxCoverageDelta, requireEmptyProposals, minWallClockDeltaMs). loopTarget reference validation: check() emits orphan entry for any loopTarget that doesn't exist as a node ID. Previously both were silent.

iter-id-command.desc: roadmap iter-id: .roadmap/iter.json counter (--increment, --reset). Canonical loop iteration number for artifact namespacing (evidence-iter-3.json). orient output includes iteration field when iter.json exists — agents get loop context in the standard orient call.

ergonomics-fixes.desc: Parameter property syntax fix (checkpoint.ts, audit.ts — Node 25 strip-only crash). checkpoint --label note-optional (synthesizes from label). orient --ready includes myClaims[] for current-batch owned nodes. Pre-commit: single orient call (3→1 spawns on failure).

phase-20-term.desc: Phase 20 complete: complete command with auto-advance, NodeSpec.ambient, convergenceCheck/loopTarget validation, iter-id, ergonomics fixes (parameter properties, note-optional checkpoint, myClaims, single orient spawn) all operational.

trail-metrics.desc: src/lib/trail-metrics.ts: roadmap-native performance metrics from trail.jsonl + claims.json. Computes per-node durations, batch wall-clock, orient-call counts, iteration number. source: "trail" sentinel distinguishes from regent-enriched metrics. transcript-metrics calls this always; agent JSONL data enriches but is never required for convergence check.

phase-21-term.desc: Phase 21 complete: trail-metrics baseline layer operational. transcript-metrics node can call computeTrailMetrics() and optionally enrich with agent JSONL data. Improvement loop is runnable without regent.

validate-cli.desc: Wire validateNode into cmdComplete + --skip-validate flag + roadmap report command in bin/roadmap.ts

scaffold-impl.desc: buildScaffold(): topologically generate typed stub files for all DAG produces, optional tsc type-check

cluster-impl.desc: buildClusters(): bipartite produces/consumes graph → connected-component clusters with coupling + critical path annotation

schedule-impl.desc: buildSchedule(): inter-cluster dep graph → wave assignment with critical path ordering

phase-24-term.desc: Phase 24 complete: DAG-as-Compiler — scaffold, cluster, schedule, unblocked surfacing all ship

fr-impl-init.desc: Phase 25 gate — existing files available as ambient context for gallery implementation

fr-compile-prompts-intent.desc:
Enhance src/lib/compile-prompts.ts: intent rules in validate[] emit annotated self-check items
with failure mode hints from evaluation history.

Currently buildVerificationChecklist() emits:
  case "intent": return `- [ ] Intent (${rule.evaluator}): "${rule.statement}"`

Required change:
  1. Add buildIntentSelfCheck(validate: ValidationRule[], nodeId: string, repoRoot: string): string
     - For each intent rule: emit "- [ ] \"${statement}\" (threshold: ${confidence}, evaluator: ${evaluator})"
     - Call readEvaluations(nodeId, repoRoot) from src/lib/intent-evaluator.ts
     - Filter by rule.statement, find last failing record (r.pass === false)
     - If found: emit "      Known failure mode: ${lastFailure.reasoning}"
     - If found && lastFailure.evidence.length: emit "      Evidence: ${lastFailure.evidence.join(", ")}"
  2. In fillTemplate(): call buildIntentSelfCheck for intent rules, append as "### Intent (self-check)" subsection
     in the Verification section, after deterministic items.
     Signature change: fillTemplate(node, env, cluster?, repoRoot?) — repoRoot optional for backward compat.
  3. compilePrompts(): pass repoRoot through to fillTemplate.

Do NOT change the existing deterministic checklist items. The intent section is additive.
Do NOT lower confidence thresholds — hints enrich prompts, they do not relax gates.

consumes: src/lib/intent-evaluator.ts (readEvaluations export)

validate.statement: Intent self-check items include failure mode hint from evaluation history when prior failures exist, but history never adjusts confidence thresholds

fr-emit-node-type.desc:
Add EmitGalleryNodeSpec type and nodeType discriminant to src/protocol.ts.

Add to protocol.ts:
  export interface EmitGalleryNodeSpec {
    id: string
    nodeType: "emit-gallery"           // discriminant, distinct from mode
    candidates: number                 // how many implementations to generate
    strategies: string[]               // e.g. ["faithful", "minimal", "robust", "budget"]
    selectionMode: "auto" | "manual"   // auto = LLM selects via Judgment
    validate: ValidationRule[]         // gate suite applied to each candidate
    produces: string[]
    deps?: string[]
    desc?: string
  }

  Add to NodeSpec<TAll, TSelf>: nodeType?: "execute" | "emit-gallery"
  (mode is already "execute" | "plan" — nodeType is a separate dispatch dimension)

  Export EmitGalleryNodeSpec from protocol.ts.

Do NOT change ValidationRule, Graph, or Orientation types. Additive only.

fr-strategies.desc:
Create src/lib/strategies/ — generation strategy definitions for emit-gallery.

Create src/lib/strategies/index.ts exporting:
  export interface StrategySpec {
    id: string                  // "faithful" | "minimal" | "robust" | "budget"
    label: string               // human-readable name
    systemPrompt: string        // injected as system prompt for the generator
    model: string               // e.g. "claude-opus-4-6" | "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"
    estimatedCostMultiplier: number  // relative cost vs baseline (1.0 = baseline)
  }
  export const STRATEGIES: StrategySpec[]
  export function getStrategy(id: string): StrategySpec

Four strategies in STRATEGIES:
  faithful: Opus, spec-literal, no embellishment. systemPrompt emphasizes exact spec adherence.
  minimal: Haiku, fewest files, simplest architecture. systemPrompt emphasizes reduction.
  robust: Opus, defensive coding, comprehensive error handling. systemPrompt emphasizes edge cases.
  budget: Haiku, same spec input as faithful. systemPrompt identical to faithful. costMultiplier ~0.15.

File: src/lib/strategies/index.ts (single file, no subdirectory needed)

validate.statement: Pareto filtering uses cardinal risk = 1 - (historicalSuccessRate × gateConvergenceRate), defaults to 1.0 on cold start, and conservative template always survives when all candidates share risk=1.0

fr-cost-estimator.desc:
Create src/lib/cost-estimator.ts — history-based cost and time estimation for gallery candidates.

Exports:
  export interface CostEstimate {
    wallClockMinutes: number
    costUSD: number
    confidence: "cold-start" | "low" | "medium" | "high"  // based on history sample size
  }

  export function estimateCost(params: {
    nodeCount: number,
    modelAllocation: TemplateParams["modelAllocation"],
    checkpointDir?: string,       // .roadmap/checkpoints/ for duration baselines
    evaluationsDir?: string,      // .roadmap/evaluations/ for gate convergence history
  }): CostEstimate

  Cost model:
    opus: $0.015/1k input tokens, ~0.5k tokens/node average
    haiku: $0.00025/1k input tokens, same node count
  Duration model:
    Read checkpoint durations from checkpointDir if available
    Fallback: 1.5 min/node for opus, 0.5 min/node for haiku
  Confidence:
    cold-start = no history files found
    low = 1-2 prior runs
    medium = 3-9 prior runs
    high = 10+ prior runs

Import TemplateParams from src/lib/gallery.ts.
No Anthropic API calls.

fr-gallery-templates.desc:
Create src/lib/gallery-templates/index.ts — four named plan templates as GalleryCandidate factories.

Exports:
  export interface TemplateDefinition {
    id: "aggressive" | "corrective" | "staged" | "budget"
    label: string
    summary: string
    parameters: TemplateParams
    buildDag(specSource: string, historyFailureClasses?: string[]): Record<string, any>
    // Returns head.json-compatible partial DAGSpec (nodes + deps, no id/init/term)
  }
  export const TEMPLATES: TemplateDefinition[]
  export function buildGallery(specSource: string, historyDir?: string): GalleryCandidate[]
    // Instantiate all 4 templates, score with estimateCost + computeRisk, return Pareto-filtered set.

Template DAG shapes:
  aggressive:  emit-gallery(4 candidates, parallel) → runtime-gate → converged
  corrective:  emit-gallery(4 candidates, serial gates) → intent → runtime-gate → converged
               preExpansion: "from-history" — adds fix nodes for known failure classes
  staged:      emit-gallery(skeleton, 3 candidates) → emit-gallery(features, 3 candidates) → test → intent → runtime-gate → converged
  budget:      emit-gallery(4 haiku candidates) → compile → test → runtime-gate → converged

Import GalleryCandidate, TemplateParams, computeRisk, paretoFilter from src/lib/gallery.ts.
Import estimateCost from src/lib/cost-estimator.ts.

validate.statement: Gallery treats maxFixPasses as a per-gate budget after selection, not a generation retry count; all-candidates-fail produces GalleryFailure with structured evidence rather than additional generation attempts

fr-blend.desc:
Create src/lib/blend.ts — multi-candidate file merging with intent-gate conflict resolution.

Exports:
  export interface BlendSpec {
    primary: string     // candidate id — base architecture source
    donors: string[]    // candidates to pull cheaper files from
  }

  export interface BlendResult {
    files: Record<string, string>  // merged file set
    substitutions: Array<{ path: string; from: string; reason: string }>
    reverted: Array<{ path: string; reason: string }>
    deterministicPass: boolean
    intentScore: string
  }

  export function blendCandidates(
    candidates: CandidateResult[],
    spec: BlendSpec,
    fileToIntents: FileToIntents,
  ): BlendResult

Algorithm:
  1. Start with primary candidate files.
  2. For each donor candidate, for each file in donor:
     a. Look up fileToIntents[path] → list of intent statements covering this file.
     b. If the donor passed ALL those intents AND donor.summary.loc < primary.summary.loc → substitute.
     c. Re-check deterministic validators (tsc pass, vitest pass) on the blended set.
        If deterministic gate breaks → revert the substitution.
  3. Re-run intent evaluation on blended result (use donor intent results where unchanged,
     mark substituted files as needing re-evaluation).
  4. If blend fails gates that all source candidates passed → discard, return primary files.

Note: files with empty fileToIntents[path] are not substitutable (conservative).
  This is by design — see FR-EMIT-GALLERY open question 5.

Import CandidateResult, FileToIntents from src/lib/emit-gallery.ts.

validate.statement: Blend substitutes donor files only when the donor passed ALL intent statements in fileToIntents[path], and reverts any substitution that breaks a deterministic gate

fr-plan-gallery-cli.desc:
Add `roadmap plan --gallery` command to bin/roadmap.ts.

New command: plan --gallery [--from <specFile>] [--select <id>] [--evaluate <json>] [--json]

Handler cmdPlanGallery():
  1. Parse --from <specFile> (defaults to first .specify/specs/**/*.md found, or empty string)
  2. Parse --select <id> to commit a previously-generated candidate
  3. Parse --evaluate <json> for LLM judgment (same Judgment schema as complete --evaluate)
  4. If no --select and no --evaluate:
     a. Call buildGallery(specSource, repoRoot + "/.roadmap/evaluations")
     b. Render ASCII table comparison (id, nodes, wallClockMinutes, costUSD, risk)
     c. Show topology diagram per candidate (compact: "emit → compile ─┬─ test ─┬─ runtime")
     d. Show recommendation (lowest risk)
     e. Output "Select [A/B/C/D]:"
  5. If --evaluate <json>:
     a. Parse Judgment[] from json
     b. Validate confidence >= 0.7 (minimum bar for plan selection)
     c. Record to .roadmap/evaluations/plan-selection.jsonl with phase: "plan-selection:<runId>"
     d. Write selected candidate dag as .roadmap/head.json (AFTER backing up existing as .roadmap/head-prev.json)
     e. Output: { selected: id, committed: true, headPath: ".roadmap/head.json" }
  6. If --select <id>: same as --evaluate but without confidence requirement (manual override)

Wire in main switch: case "plan": if (args.includes("--gallery")) ...

Import buildGallery from src/lib/gallery-templates/index.ts
Import estimateCost from src/lib/cost-estimator.ts

After adding the command, run: npx tsc --noEmit to verify no type errors.

fr-emit-gallery-cli.desc:
Add `roadmap emit --gallery` command and emit-gallery complete dispatch to bin/roadmap.ts.

Two changes to bin/roadmap.ts:

1. New command: emit --gallery [--candidates N] [--from <spec>] [--select <id>] [--blend <A+B>] [--evaluate <json>] [--json]
   Handler cmdEmitGallery():
   a. Parse flags
   b. Load DAG, find emit-gallery nodes (nodeType === "emit-gallery")
   c. Call runGallery({ nodeSpec, strategies: STRATEGIES, workDir: repoRoot+"/.roadmap/gallery/" })
   d. Render scorecard ASCII table (Candidate, Files, LOC, tsc, vitest, build, intent, cost)
   e. Show: "Deterministic survivors: ...", "Intent survivors: ..."
   f. If --blend <A+B>: call blendCandidates(), re-evaluate, show blend result
   g. If --evaluate <json>: parse Judgment, record to .roadmap/evaluations/emit-selection.jsonl,
      copy winning candidate files to main working tree, commit
   h. If --select <id>: same as --evaluate without confidence check

2. In cmdComplete handler, add dispatch on node.nodeType:
   After loading nodeSpec, check: if ((nodeSpec as any).nodeType === "emit-gallery") {
     await handleEmitGalleryComplete(nodeSpec, repoRoot)
     return  // skip standard validation
   }
   handleEmitGalleryComplete() = runGallery + auto-select best intent survivor + commit files.

Wire in main switch: case "emit": if (args.includes("--gallery")) ...

Import runGallery from src/lib/emit-gallery.ts
Import blendCandidates from src/lib/blend.ts
Import STRATEGIES from src/lib/strategies/index.ts

After adding both changes, run: npx tsc --noEmit to verify no type errors.

fr-gallery-tests.desc:
Tests for plan gallery: types, Pareto filtering, risk formula, cost estimation, template generation.

File: tests/gallery.test.ts

Test suites:
  computeRisk():
    - Both rates 1.0 → risk 0.0
    - Both rates 0.0 → risk 1.0 (cold start: default to 0 → risk = 1.0)
    - Mixed: (0.8 * 0.9) → risk = 1 - 0.72 = 0.28

  paretoFilter():
    - All candidates distinct → all survive (no domination)
    - B dominates A (cheaper AND faster AND safer) → A removed
    - Partial domination (cheaper but riskier) → both survive
    - Cold start (all risk=1.0): conservative template (lowest cost estimate) survives
    - Result always has 1–4 candidates

  estimateCost():
    - Cold start (no historyDir): confidence="cold-start", uses fallback rates
    - Opus-all allocation: higher cost, lower or equal wallClockMinutes
    - Haiku allocation: lower cost

  buildGallery():
    - Returns 3-4 candidates (Pareto filtered)
    - Each has id, label, estimates, gateProfile
    - Contains "corrective" template when history failure classes present

Import from src/lib/gallery.ts and src/lib/cost-estimator.ts.

fr-emit-gallery-tests.desc:
Tests for emit gallery: buildFileToIntents, blendCandidates, convergence protocol, strategy specs.

File: tests/emit-gallery.test.ts

Test suites:
  buildFileToIntents():
    - Intent rule with contextPaths → inverted index
    - Multiple rules with overlapping paths → all statements included
    - Rule without contextPaths → excluded (conservative)

  blendCandidates():
    - Donor file passes all fileToIntents intents AND is smaller → substituted
    - Donor file fails an intent → not substituted
    - File not in fileToIntents (empty entry) → not substitutable
    - Substitution breaks deterministic gate → reverted
    - All-donors-fail → returns primary candidate files unchanged

  ConvergenceConfig:
    - runGallery with 4 strategies → GalleryRunResult with 4 candidates
    - All candidates fail same intent → GalleryFailure in result.failures
    - GalleryFailure includes bestConfidence, threshold, diagnosis

  StrategySpec (from src/lib/strategies/index.ts):
    - getStrategy("faithful") returns StrategySpec with model containing "opus"
    - getStrategy("budget") returns StrategySpec with lower estimatedCostMultiplier
    - getStrategy("unknown") throws
    - STRATEGIES has exactly 4 entries

Import from src/lib/emit-gallery.ts, src/lib/blend.ts, src/lib/strategies/index.ts.

fr-intent-selfcheck-tests.desc:
Tests for compile-prompts intent self-check with history enrichment.

File: tests/compile-prompts-intent.test.ts
(Extend or create alongside existing compile-prompts tests.)

Test suites:
  buildIntentSelfCheck() — no history:
    - Intent rule with no prior failures → emits statement + threshold, no hint

  buildIntentSelfCheck() — with failing history:
    - Prior failure exists → emits "Known failure mode: ${reasoning}"
    - Prior failure with evidence → emits "Evidence: ..."
    - Prior failure then pass (most recent is pass) → no hint emitted
    - Multiple statements → only matching statement gets hint

  compilePrompts() integration:
    - Node with intent rule + history → compiled prompt contains "Known failure mode"
    - Node with intent rule + no history → compiled prompt contains statement but no hint
    - Deterministic checklist items still present alongside intent self-check

Use tmp directory for fake .roadmap/evaluations/ records.
Import fillTemplate, compilePrompts from src/lib/compile-prompts.ts.
Import recordEvaluation, judgmentToRecord from src/lib/intent-evaluator.ts (for test setup).

phase-25-term.desc: Phase 25 complete: gallery system ships — plan gallery, emit gallery, compile-prompts intent self-check, blend, strategies
